user_query:
I'm organizing a focused ethics check-in for our bioengineering project and need help coordinating communication and prep materials using the local inputs.

Inputs (read-only):
- input/topics.csv — discussion topics with category, urgency, stakeholders, and a guiding question.
- input/contacts.json — names/emails for key roles (PI, Biosafety Officer, Lab Manager, Team Lead, Ethics Club Chair) and the ethics discussion mailing list alias.

Please produce the following and save them exactly at the specified paths:

1) Draft an email to invite our PI to a meeting (out/emails/professor_invite_email.txt):
   - Begin with three header lines exactly in this order:
     To: <PI email from contacts.json>
     Cc: <Ethics Club Chair email from contacts.json>
     Subject: Meeting on <N> High-Priority Ethics Topics
     where N is the count of rows in input/topics.csv with urgency == High.
   - After a blank line, include:
     - An opening greeting addressing the PI by name (from contacts.json).
     - A one-sentence purpose/request for a 45-minute meeting to discuss high-priority ethical concerns.
     - A bullet list (one per line, prefixed with "- ") of the High-urgency topic titles exactly as they appear in input/topics.csv.
     - A concise closing line indicating you will share an agenda and notes template.

2) Draft a student-facing announcement to the ethics discussion list (out/messages/club_announcement.txt):
   - Header lines (in order):
     To: <group_aliases.ethics_discussion_list from contacts.json>
     Subject: Input Requested on <M> Medium-Priority Topics
     where M is the count of rows with urgency == Medium.
   - Body with a one-sentence invitation for input, followed by a bullet list ("- ") of all Medium-urgency topic titles from input/topics.csv, and a closing note asking for replies before the meeting.

3) Prepare a meeting agenda (out/meeting/agenda.md):
   - A "High-Priority Topics" section that lists every High-urgency item with for each item: Topic, Category, Stakeholders (as listed, semicolon-separated), and Guiding question (from the CSV).
   - A "Medium-Priority Topics" section that includes the first two Medium-urgency items in CSV order with the same fields.
   - High items must appear before Medium items.

4) Prepare a meeting notes template (out/meeting/notes_template.md):
   - Mirror the agenda items in the same order.
   - For each item, include placeholders labeled: "Discussion Notes:", "Decisions:", and "Follow-ups:" so a scribe can fill them in during/after the meeting.

5) Generate initial action items for High-urgency topics (out/meeting/action_items.json):
   - A JSON array with one object per High-urgency row from input/topics.csv, each with the fields:
     - "topic": the topic title exactly as in the CSV
     - "owner_email": determine by scanning that row’s "stakeholders" left-to-right and selecting the first stakeholder role that exactly matches a contact.role in input/contacts.json; set to that contact’s email. If no stakeholder role matches any contact.role, set "owner_email" to "TBD".
     - "status": "pending"
     - "due_date": null
     - "source": "input/topics.csv"

Consistency constraints:
- The count N in the PI email subject must equal the number of High-urgency rows in input/topics.csv.
- The count M in the announcement subject must equal the number of Medium-urgency rows in input/topics.csv.
- The bullet lists in the email and announcement must contain exactly the corresponding topic titles from input/topics.csv and no others.
- The agenda and notes template must reflect the same titles and ordering rules described above.

Do not modify the input files. Make sure all five output files are created at the specified paths. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
